vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. The pots are present, but the biological specimens are either absent, dormant, or in a state of advanced decay.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; pot contains only soil/debris.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; pot contains only soil/debris.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen is absent or completely withered.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit figurine present, but no plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Consistently dark, damp-appearing substrate across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshells or mineral deposits) in P3 and P4.
biome_observations.desk_environment: Presence of a white cylindrical object (likely a supplement bottle or container) introduced in the mid-sequence.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-5: Minor shift in desk objects; plant matter already absent.
temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Introduction of a white cylindrical object near the yellow pot (P1).
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: The white cylindrical object remains; no biological recovery observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered specimens have suffered total systemic loss. The environment appears to be a 'dead' setup with no active photosynthesis occurring.
anomalies: Introduction of a white cylindrical object (supplement bottle) near the yellow pot.
Presence of white, crushed debris on the soil surface of P3/P4.
Complete absence of expected botanical specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a critical failure of the botanical biome. Across the entire chronological sequence, there is no evidence of the registered plants (String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, Pothos, Silver Guest). The pots contain only soil and, in some cases, inorganic debris. The environment is static, and the lack of any green biomass suggests the plants have either been removed or have completely perished prior to the start of this observation window.
